title: Consumer rollout amplified retry pressure and backlog
failure_mechanism: A consumer rollout during resource pressure triggered retry amplification and dependency contention, reducing effective consumer throughput and growing the notification queue.
mitigation_summary: The incident stabilized after concurrent retry pressure fell and consumer throughput returned; a historical match does not establish the same dependency is at fault now.
timeline_summary: An unrelated rollout coincided with declining consumer throughput. Increased retries and dependency latency appeared alongside a growing notification queue.
narrative_safe: RelayOps notification workers lost effective throughput after a rollout while dependency latency increased. The queue backlog may resemble a pure database-delay incident, but the deployment and retry pattern make that an unsafe assumption without verification.
